ex.en: He is too lazy to clean his room.
ex.ko: 그는 너무 게을러서 방을 청소하지 않는다.

ex.en: She is a good leader.
A leaf fell from the tree.
She learned to dance from her sister.
What time does your train leave?
Turn left at the corner.
He broke his leg while skiing.
He took driving lessons last month.
Please let me help you.
She wrote a letter to her grandmother.
He studies at the library every day.
She has a happy life.
The light was soft in the evening.
Do you like this song?
She placed lilies on the table.
Please stand in line.
The lion is the king of beasts.
She made a shopping list.
Listen to this song.
She has a little brother.
They live in Seoul.

We watch TV in the living room.
He felt lonely after moving to a new city.
ex.ko: 그는 새 도시로 이사한 후 외로움을 느꼈다.

ex.en: It was a long trip.
She had a look of triumph.

enOverride: a large amount or number of something

ex.en: She has a lot of friends.
I love French food.
What a lovely garden!
Good luck on your exam!
He had a lucky escape from the accident.
We had lunch together at noon.
This machine washes clothes.
She reads a fashion magazine every month.
He checked his mail this morning.
She made a cake for the party.
There were two women and six men on the bus.
ex.ko: 버스에는 여자 두 명과 남자 여섯 명이 있었다.

ex.en: She has many friends.
He looked at the map to find the way.
ex.ko: 그는 길을 찾기 위해 지도를 봤다.

ex.en: Spring begins in March.
He always carries matches to light his pipe.
ex.ko: 그는 항상 파이프에 불을 붙이려고 성냥을 가지고 다닌다.

ex.en: She is good at math.
There are several matters to attend to.
ex.ko: 처리해야 할 몇 가지 문제가 있다.

enOverride: used to say that something is possible, or to give permission

ex.en: It may rain later.
Her birthday is in May.
Maybe she will call tomorrow.

enOverride: used to refer to the speaker, as the object of a verb or preposition

ex.en: Can you help me?
They enjoyed a nice meal together.
You never understand what I mean!
ex.ko: 당신은 내가 무슨 뜻인지 전혀 이해하지 못해요!

ex.en: She doesn't eat meat.
He takes medicine for his headache.
Let's meet at the cafe tomorrow.
The meeting will be held next Monday.
ex.ko: 회의는 다음 주 월요일에 열릴 것이다.

ex.en: She has a good memory for names.
Merry Christmas to you all!
He sent her a short message.
She sat in the middle seat.
He drinks a glass of milk every morning.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 아침 우유 한 잔을 마신다.

ex.en: Keep an open mind.

enOverride: used to show that something belongs to the speaker

ex.en: This umbrella is mine.
Wait a minute, please.
Miss Kim is my teacher.
I miss my family a lot.

enOverride: able to move or be moved easily; often short for 'mobile phone'

ex.en: She checked her mobile for messages.
He lost his mobile phone yesterday.
My mom cooks dinner every night.
ex.ko: 우리 엄마는 매일 밤 저녁을 요리하신다.

ex.en: The moment he arrived, the party began.
She called out, "Mommy!"
School starts again on Monday.
He saved a lot of money.
The monkey climbed the tree quickly.
He paid the bill last month.
The moon was bright last night.

enOverride: a greater amount or number of something

ex.en: Can I have some more coffee?
ex.ko: 커피 좀 더 마실 수 있을까요?

ex.en: I spent the morning running errands.
Most students passed the exam.
She is the mother of three children.
They climbed the mountain together.
A mouse ran across the kitchen floor.
ex.ko: 쥐 한 마리가 부엌 바닥을 가로질러 뛰어갔다.

ex.en: He stuffed his mouth with candy.
They decided to move to a new city.
They went to a movie every Saturday night.
ex.ko: 그들은 매주 토요일 밤에 영화를 보러 갔다.

ex.en: She's much better now.

enOverride: a British informal word for mother

ex.en: She lives with her mum.
She listens to music every day.
He wants to be a musician.

enOverride: used to say that something is necessary or certain

ex.en: You must finish your homework first.

enOverride: used to show that something belongs to the speaker

ex.en: This is my bag.
His name is George.
What is your nationality?
The station is near my house.
She wore a scarf around her neck.
I need some help with this.
Our neighbor is very kind.
I have never been to Japan.

They awaited news of the outcome.
He reads the newspaper every morning.
She is a nice person.
They talked all night.
She has nine pencils.
He turned nineteen last week.
My grandfather is ninety years old.
No, thank you.
He enjoyed the street noises.
She has a small nose.
He does not speak French.
She left a note on the table.
He writes in his notebook every day.
There is nothing in the box.
Thanksgiving is in November.
I have to go now.
Can I have your phone number?
The nurse took care of the patient.
It's three o'clock now.
The festival is held every October.

enOverride: used to show a connection between two things, such as belonging or origin

ex.en: This is the capital of Korea.
The oven is off.
He rented an office in the new building.
The officer helped the lost child.
ex.ko: 그 경찰관은 길을 잃은 아이를 도왔다.

ex.en: We often meet over a cup of coffee.
ex.ko: 우리는 자주 커피 한 잔을 마시며 만난다.

ex.en: Is it OK if I sit here?
His mother is very old.
The Olympics are held every four years.
The book is on the table.
I ran into her once at the mall.
ex.ko: 나는 쇼핑몰에서 한 번 그녀와 우연히 마주쳤다.

ex.en: She has only one brother.
She is the only child in her family.
Mary opened the car door.
We watched an opera last weekend.
